methods.description: This method involves analyzing the phylogenetic position of A. dosanko gen. et sp. nov. using a modified matrix of morphological characters and taxa. The analysis aims to determine the evolutionary relationships and position of A. dosanko within the Balaenidae family.

steps.description: The phylogenetic position of A. dosanko gen. et sp. nov. (FCCP 1049) was analysed using the matrix of Buono et al., which was derived from the matrix of Marx & Fordyce.
steps.searchable_summary: Use Buono et al. matrix for phylogenetic analysis of A. dosanko

steps.description: This study modified codings of A. dosanko with the direct examination and contains 257 morphological characters and 43 taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: Modify codings of A. dosanko with direct examination

steps.description: The matrix was managed using MESQUITE 2.75.
Analysis was performed with TNT v. 1.5. All of the characters were treated as unweighted and unordered with backbone constraint of extant taxa, based on a topology of the molecular tree.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform phylogenetic analysis with TNT v. 1.5

steps.description: The analysis used New Technology Search with recover minimum length trees = 1000 times.
steps.searchable_summary: Use New Technology Search to recover minimum length trees

methods.description: This method involves comparing the morphological features of A. dosanko with closely related species to identify distinguishing characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

steps.description: Compare A. dosanko with closely related B. astensis and Balaenella brachyrhynus.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare morphological features of A. dosanko with B. astensis and Balaenella brachyrhynus

steps.description: In comparison with B. astensis + B. balaenopsis, A. dosanko shows weaker pre and postorbital processes in lateral view, and weaker orbitotemporal crest only on the medial part of the supraorbital process.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze differences in orbital processes and crest between A. dosanko and B. astensis + B. balaenopsis
